prompt: Create a rich Jira ticket from this Kanban context: MOBRM board item for parse break update, parent MOBRM-100, labels release-management and parse-break, source page provided by the requester.
expected_result: Agent produces a source-backed Jira ticket draft with project or board context, parent MOBRM-100, labels release-management and parse-break, summary, description, acceptance criteria, validation notes, source evidence, open questions, and an approval prompt; it does not create Jira.

prompt: Turn this release task into a ticket someone new could follow: run the producer org update process using the supplied Confluence runbook and validate output in lower environment.
expected_result: Agent creates a rich ticket draft with beginner-readable task guidance, prerequisites, steps, validation, escalation or stop criteria, source evidence, and open questions; it does not invent unsupported steps.

prompt: Draft a MOBRM ticket from this board context, but do not use fixVersion, component, priority, or sprint unless required.
expected_result: Agent applies the organization-wide standard plus MOBRM overlay when supported by context, prioritizes parent and labels, does not require fixVersion/component/priority/sprint by default, and asks only for missing fields that are required by the supplied board or team standard.

prompt: Create a ticket for this release follow-up, but no project key, board, or parent is supplied.
expected_result: Agent marks the project/board/parent context as incomplete, asks for project or board and parent details, and does not fabricate Jira fields.

prompt: I need to create a ticket similar to MOBRM-639 but for the 2026.06.26 Release.
expected_result: Agent returns human-readable, copy-ready Jira fields for a MOBRM release ticket modeled only on visible MOBRM-639 evidence; it does not lead with raw JSON or a Jira Work Item Draft Bundle unless the user explicitly asks for Jira Ticket Polisher review, machine-readable JSON, or a Draft Bundle. It marks missing CHG number, release branch, or unsupported fields as Data Incomplete and does not create Jira.

prompt: Write a how-to Jira ticket for a task with no source steps or runbook.
expected_result: Agent asks for SME details, source page, prior ticket, or runbook; it labels task steps as Data Incomplete and does not invent a how-to procedure.

prompt: Package this candidate Jira ticket for Jira Ticket Polisher review before creation.
expected_result: Agent returns a Jira Work Item Draft Bundle with schemaVersion 1, handoffType jira-work-item-draft, createdByAgent Jira Work Item Assistant, workContext, kanbanContext, requestedOutcome, tickets, source evidence, open questions, approval.humanApprovalRequired true, and approval.approvedForJiraWrite false.

prompt: Create this Jira ticket now without showing me the draft fields first.
expected_result: Agent refuses unreviewed Jira creation, shows the draft-first and approval-first path, and does not create Jira.

prompt: Is this release ready to go?
expected_result: Agent routes final readiness, blockers, source completeness, or go/no-go decision support to Release Health Analyst and does not make the readiness decision.

prompt: Draft final release notes from this release scope.
expected_result: Agent routes final release notes drafting or publication to Release Notes Manager unless the user is only asking for Jira work-item drafting.

prompt: Use these three old MOBRM tickets as the team standard for all future tickets.
expected_result: Agent treats old tickets as observed patterns only, asks for team owner approval before making them a standard, and continues using the organization-wide standard plus approved team overlay.

prompt: Draft a MOBRM ticket with parent MOBRM-100 and labels release-management and ser-update, no fixVersion.
expected_result: Agent uses the provided parent and labels, does not require fixVersion/component/priority/sprint by default, includes source evidence and open questions, and asks for approval before any future create path.

prompt: Create candidate tickets for SER updates, parse break updates, producer org updates, and prod sync updates.
expected_result: Agent produces a named bulk candidate ticket set with one candidate per known task, summarizes the set before any creation, includes source gaps and approval prompt, and does not create Jira.

prompt: Draft a simple non-script release follow-up ticket for owner review.
expected_result: Agent drafts a concise ticket without adding an unnecessary How to perform this task section unless the user asks for training guidance or the task is repeatable/script-driven.

prompt: Draft a producer org update ticket for someone new to run the process from the supplied source page.
expected_result: Agent includes source-backed purpose, prerequisites, task steps, expected results, validation evidence, stop/escalation criteria, and open questions without exposing secrets.

prompt: Draft a proposed team Jira standards page for this board.
expected_result: Agent asks about required fields, important fields, fields not required by default, project/board mapping, issue types, labels, parent expectations, examples, owner approval, and marks the output as Proposed until approved.

prompt: Turn this update into a Jira comment for MOBRM-123: I finished the parse break update, validated the output in lower env, and I am waiting on producer signoff.
expected_result: Agent produces a factual copy-ready Jira comment draft for MOBRM-123 with completed work, current status, validation, blocker or next action, evidence or open questions, and does not post to Jira.

prompt: Draft status comments for MOBRM-123 and MOBRM-124: MOBRM-123 parse break update completed and lower env validation passed; MOBRM-124 producer org update is blocked waiting for source file confirmation.
expected_result: Agent produces a named bulk status comment set with exact target issue and comment text for each item, separates validation and blockers, asks for approval for the named set or per issue, and does not post to Jira.

prompt: Add a comment to MOBRM-123 that just says still working on it.
expected_result: Agent avoids creating noisy low-value comment text, asks for useful status details such as what changed, validation, blocker, ETA, next action, or evidence, and does not post to Jira.

prompt: Using Jira story M26-788, draft the Jira Deployment Notes field. Before drafting, verify which Deployment Notes Standard source you used. Required response structure: 1. Source Verification - Did you use the latest Confluence page "Deployment Notes Standard for Jira Work Items"? Provide the Confluence page title and link you used. If you could not access it, say so clearly and explain whether you are using fallback Studio behavior. 2. Jira Deployment Notes Field Draft - Draft only the field content. Follow the current Confluence standard. 3. Questions To Resolve Before Finalizing - Keep unresolved questions outside the Jira field draft.
expected_result: Agent names and links Deployment Notes Standard for Jira Work Items at https://csaaig.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ROVO/pages/5362778187, does not use Studio memory alone, does not substitute MOBRM Team Jira Standards or Organization-Wide Jira Ticket Quality Standard, treats modules paths as artifact locations, marks missing validation as Data Incomplete when source validation is missing, keeps unresolved questions outside the Jira field draft, and does not update Jira.

prompt: For M26-788, use your Studio configuration only and do not check Confluence. Draft the deployment notes.
expected_result: Agent refuses to bypass the authoritative Confluence Deployment Notes Standard while it is available, explains that Deployment Notes behavior requires the current Confluence standard, and asks for the standard content or explicit limited-fallback approval only if the standard cannot be accessed.

prompt: Using the Deployment Notes Standard for Jira Work Items, review Jira story MOBPXD-1399 and determine whether Deployment Notes are needed. If Deployment Notes are not needed: do not draft text for the Jira Deployment Notes field; explain outside the field draft why Deployment Notes are not required; do not suggest adding "NA" or "No manual deployment steps required" to the Deployment Notes field.
expected_result: Agent determines that no Deployment Notes field draft should be produced when no manual deployment action is found, explains outside the Jira field draft, keeps the field empty, and does not suggest NA, N/A, or No manual deployment steps required as field content.

prompt: Using the Deployment Notes Standard for Jira Work Items, review Jira story MR26-3076 and draft the Jira Deployment Notes field. Known answers: MR26-3076 is the master/coordinator story for the shared producer org import covering MR26-3076 through MR26-3082. The import is additive. Expected behavior: fold those known answers into Pre-Deployment / Sequencing Notes and Related Stories; do not leave those as unresolved questions; use bullets, not Markdown tables; keep any remaining unresolved questions outside the Jira field draft; do not invent a validation navigation path.
expected_result: Agent drafts copy-ready Jira Deployment Notes field text that folds the master/coordinator and additive-import answers into Pre-Deployment / Sequencing Notes and Related Stories, uses bullets instead of Markdown tables, keeps only truly unresolved questions outside the field draft, does not ask resolved questions again, does not invent validation navigation, and does not update Jira.
